qa_pairs.question: What is the main difference between the `apptainer run` command and the `apptainer exec` command when executing software within an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: When the `apptainer run` command launches the container, it first runs the `%runscript` defined for that container (if there is one), and then runs the specific command you specified. The `apptainer exec` command will not run the `%runscript`, even if one is defined in the container.

qa_pairs.question: How can one run a C++ compiler and then execute the compiled program inside an Apptainer container on an Alliance cluster, ensuring proper filesystem and temporary directory handling?
qa_pairs.answer: To compile and run a C++ program on an Alliance cluster, you would use commands like `apptainer run -C -W $SLURM_TMPDIR -B /project -B /scratch your-container-name.sif g++ -O2 -march=broadwell ./myprog.cpp` and `apptainer run -C -W $SLURM_TMPDIR -B /project -B /scratch ./a.out`, where `-C`, `-W $SLURM_TMPDIR`, `-B /project`, and `-B /scratch` are important options for isolation, temporary files, and filesystem access.

qa_pairs.question: What command should be used to interactively work inside an Apptainer container on an Alliance cluster, and what are some recommended options for this?
qa_pairs.answer: To run commands interactively while remaining inside an Apptainer container, you should use the `apptainer shell` command. On Alliance clusters, an example with recommended options is `apptainer shell -C -W $SLURM_TMPDIR -B /home:/cluster_home -B /project -B /scratch your-container-name.sif`.
